title: How Lasers Differ From Flashlights
script_summary: Exploring the key differences between lasers and flashlights in terms of light coherence.

segments.voiceover: What if I told you not all light is created equal?
Lasers emit coherent light, a precise beam that stays focused over long distances.
Flashlights? They scatter light in all directions, losing intensity quickly.
This coherence means lasers can cut through materials or send signals far away.
So, next time you see a laser, remember, it’s not just strong light; it’s focused energy.
Curious for more science facts? Hit follow and light up your knowledge!
